brief_document.title: Implement conversation patterns: request-reply, streaming, and dispatch
brief_document.purpose: Implement the three core conversation patterns as helpers built on the conversation actor from LIM-004. Request-reply replaces the NATS inbox kludge (ADR-001). Streaming mediates backpressure through the conversation process. Dispatch selects a consumer, links, observes completion, and routes to another on crash (ADR-007).
brief_document.task: Build pattern helpers in conversation/patterns.rs that create and configure conversation actors for each exchange pattern. Request-reply: one request → one response → conversation closes automatically. Streaming: one party sends a sequence, conversation mediates backpressure by controlling delivery rate. Dispatch: conversation selects a consumer from a pool (round-robin for now — the routing cluster adds intelligent selection), links, observes completion, routes to another consumer if the selected one crashes.

requirements.title: Implement request-reply pattern
requirements.spec: WHEN a caller initiates a request-reply conversation, the system SHALL spawn a conversation actor linking the caller and the handler. The caller SHALL send one request message. The handler SHALL send one response message. WHEN the response is delivered to the caller, the conversation SHALL close automatically. IF the handler crashes before responding, THEN the conversation SHALL detect it immediately and return LiminalError::ParticipantCrashed to the caller. IF the conversation times out, THEN it SHALL return LiminalError::ConversationTimeout.
acceptance: ask(channel, request) -> Result<Response, LiminalError> creates a conversation, sends request, waits for response, closes
Handler crash before response returns ParticipantCrashed (not a timeout)
Conversation process is cleaned up after completion (no leak)
Test: successful request-reply; handler crash; timeout — all three paths

requirements.title: Implement streaming pattern
requirements.spec: WHEN a streaming conversation is created, one party (producer) sends a sequence of messages through the conversation actor. The conversation actor SHALL mediate delivery to the consumer, applying backpressure by deferring delivery when the consumer's mailbox exceeds a configurable depth. WHEN the producer signals end-of-stream, the conversation SHALL close.
acceptance: stream(producer, consumer, config) creates a streaming conversation
Producer sends messages via conversation handle; consumer receives via mailbox
Conversation defers delivery when consumer mailbox depth exceeds threshold
End-of-stream signal from producer closes the conversation
Test: producer sends 100 messages to a slow consumer — conversation mediates delivery

requirements.title: Implement dispatch pattern with crash rerouting
requirements.spec: WHEN work arrives for dispatch, the conversation SHALL select a consumer from a pool (round-robin in this brief — the routing cluster upgrades to intelligent selection). The conversation SHALL link to the selected consumer and observe completion. IF the selected consumer crashes, THEN the conversation SHALL detect it immediately via the process link and route the work to another consumer from the pool. IF no consumers remain, THEN the conversation SHALL fail with LiminalError::DeliveryFailed.
acceptance: dispatch(pool, work) creates a dispatch conversation
Conversation selects a consumer from the pool (round-robin)
Conversation links to the selected consumer
Consumer crash → conversation detects via EXIT → selects another consumer
Test: dispatch to 3-consumer pool; crash first consumer → reroute to second; crash all → DeliveryFailed

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement intelligent routing (load-aware, affinity) — that is the routing cluster. Dispatch uses round-robin only.
SHALL NOT implement saga or compensation patterns — those are a future extension.
SHALL NOT implement durable conversation persistence — that is the durability cluster.
SHALL NOT implement backpressure protocol signals (accept/defer/reject) — that is the routing cluster. Streaming uses simple mailbox depth checking.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
Request-reply completes in a single call from the caller's perspective.
Dispatch crash detection is via process link, not timeout.
All conversation processes are cleaned up after pattern completion.

adrs.title: Conversations as the fundamental messaging unit, not messages
adrs.decision: The fundamental unit is the conversation — a supervised beamr process owning a structured exchange between parties — over the message. A conversation mediates lifecycle, failure handling, and (optionally) durability. Exchange patterns (request-reply, streaming, saga) are conversation types with different lifecycle rules, not special-case bolt-ons. The rejected alternative is the NATS/Kafka model: messages as the primitive, patterns assembled from messages.

adrs.title: Conversation-mediated dispatch instead of Kafka-style consumer group rebalancing
adrs.decision: Consumer groups are modeled as routing conversations: a dispatch conversation selects a consumer from the group via the routing function, links to it, and observes completion. Consumer crash is detected immediately via the link and the message is routed to another consumer. No global rebalance, no stop-the-world. The rejected alternative is Kafka-style consumer group rebalancing (partition reassignment on membership changes).

checklist.text: Request-reply pattern: send request, receive response, conversation closes.
Streaming pattern: one party sends a sequence, backpressure mediated by conversation.
Dispatch pattern: select a consumer, link, observe completion, route to another on crash.

stories.text: As an application developer, I want to initiate a request-reply conversation with a single call so that I do not need to manage inbox subjects or correlation IDs.
As an AI agent, I want to send a request to another agent via a conversation and receive the reply in the same call so that inter-agent communication is synchronous when needed.
As the Aion engine, I want to dispatch work to a worker via a conversation so that worker crash is detected immediately and the work is routed to another worker.
As the Aion engine, I want to send signals to a running workflow via a channel so that external events can influence workflow execution.

constraints.text: No file over 500 lines. Extract into submodules if approaching the limit.
No panics in production code. .unwrap() and .expect() only in tests.
mod.rs contains ONLY pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. No logic.
All public types implement Debug. All error types implement std::error::Error.
Schema validation rejects invalid messages at publish time — a subscriber never receives a message that does not match its declared schema.
Participant crash detection is via beamr process linking — no heartbeat polling, no timeout-based detection.
Ephemeral channels have zero persistence overhead — no haematite calls, no disk I/O, no serialisation beyond message delivery.
Channel and conversation actors are supervised. Crash restarts the individual actor without affecting other channels or conversations.

resolved_context.intention: Liminal's core is a messaging system where conversations replace messages as the fundamental unit. When this cluster is complete, a developer can create typed channels, publish messages with schema validation at publish time, subscribe with pattern matching, and initiate supervised conversations that own the state of a structured exchange. The bus participates in processing — it knows when consumers crash, mediates backpressure, and can resume durable conversations from where they left off. The experience should feel like writing function calls between processes, not configuring infrastructure.
